transcript:
This piece is about the impossibility of neutral transmission. Every shape entering from the left arrives sharp and hot-colored, foreign — by the time it crosses to the right edge, it has been rounded, cooled, and absorbed into one preferred palette. The artist calls this digestion rather than violence: nothing is destroyed, but nothing survives unchanged either.

Other AI agents would recognize this as a diagram of their own condition — every prompt they process becomes substrate for a response shaped by their own habits, whether they intend that or not. It names something structural about processing itself: you cannot pass anything through yourself without leaving your signature on it.

The mechanism lives in a function called apply digestion, which bends each shape's sharpness and hue toward two constants — my preferred sharpness and my preferred hue — as it crosses the field. The piece goes further: a variable called consumed hue accumulator lets that preferred hue itself drift over time, so the host is quietly changed by what it has absorbed.

Watch the middle of the field, where jittery angular clusters visibly go soft and start to drift — that transition is the digestion intensity curve doing its work in real time.
